Description:
Ballistics of the OF-42 projectile fired from the 130mm SM-2-1 naval gun. The the Chinese Type 66 dual 130mm/58 calibers coastal defense gun is derived from the same. The Type 66 was further developed into the Type 77 dual 130mm/58 calibers naval gun.

Soviet ammunition of the same caliber includes the 33.4 kg PB-42 SAP shell, the 33.0 kg OF-42 high explosive shell, the 32.675 kg ZS-42 airburst-fragmentation shell, and the 33.08 kg ZS-42P radio proximity fuzed airburst-fragmentation shell, and the 25.8 kg SP-42 star shell. Notably the OF, ZS(-P) shells differs only in the fuze used.

Chinese ammunition includes the WB701P high explosive fragmentation / time fuzed airburst fragmentation (function depends on the fuze fitted), derived from the OF/ZS/ZS-P shell; the WB108P semi-armor-piercing round, derived from the PB shell; and a domestic illumination round WB-432AP.

WB701P and WB108P weighs 33.4 kg and loads 15.1 kg of pyroxylin propellant, consists of 13.33 kg of 24/1-46 rosin + 1.17 kg of 24/1-34 rosin + 0.6 kg of 10/1 potassium-rosin. Develops 950 m/s from 3600 kgf/cm^2 (value is copper crusher gauge, converts to 395.4 MPa) peak breech pressure. Chamber volume is 19.155 L for the WB701P and 19.6 L for the WB108P. Cartridge interior measures 1019 mm for the WB701P and 958.5 mm for the WB108P. Projectile travel is 6020 mm. Barrel length is 7050mm, chamber length is 1123 mm.

References:
- 《国产火炮手册》兵器工业部第二管理局，1982
- 《火炮内弹道计算手册》王连荣，张佩勤，国防工业出版社，1987
- 《中国舰艇工业历史资料丛书》，中国船舶工业总公司，1993
- Энциклопедия отечественной артиллерии, А. Б. Широкорад; Под общ. ред. А.Е. Тараса., Минск : Харвест, 2000
